description_long: This is a military-style attack helicopter with a streamlined, angular fuselage painted in a green and brown camouflage pattern. It features a tandem cockpit with large, angular windows and a prominent nose-mounted sensor or targeting system. The helicopter is equipped with a coaxial dual-rotor system, eliminating the need for a tail rotor, and has multiple hardpoints under the stub wings loaded with missiles and rocket pods. The tail section is angular and compact, bearing a red star insignia, and the landing gear consists of robust, fixed skids. The overall design suggests a heavily armed, agile aircraft suitable for urban or battlefield environments.
description: A camouflaged, heavily armed attack helicopter with a dual-rotor system, tandem cockpit, and fixed landing skids.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the fuselage, tandem cockpit, stub wings with mounted weapons, and the dual-rotor system.
description_view_1: Front view highlighting the narrow profile, cockpit windows, nose sensor, and the symmetrical arrangement of weapons under the wings.
description_view_2: Opposite side view similar to view 0, showing the tail section, cockpit, and weaponry from the other side.
description_view_3: Top-down view displaying the coaxial rotor blades, weapon hardpoints, and the overall compact, angular shape of the helicopter.
attribute_rotor_blades: There are two sets of long, straight, black-and-gray main rotor blades arranged in a coaxial configuration. Each set appears to have three blades, with a slight taper and no visible markings.
attribute_tail_rotor: Not visible; the helicopter uses a coaxial rotor system and does not have a conventional tail rotor.
attribute_landing_skids: The landing skids are robust, straight, and painted in a dark color, likely black or dark gray. They are fixed to the fuselage with angled supports and appear to be made of metal.
attribute_cockpit_windows: The cockpit has multiple large, angular windows with a slight tint, framed in black. The windows wrap around the front and sides, providing wide visibility for the crew.
